title: [Bug]: Release workflow passes SHA256SUMS to PyPI publisher
summary: The release workflow grouped the checksum manifest with package distributions, causing the PyPI publisher to treat `SHA256SUMS` as an uploadable distribution.
observed: The v0.4.0 release workflow failed in the PyPI publish job with an invalid distribution format error for `SHA256SUMS`.
expected: PyPI publishing should receive only `.whl` and `.tar.gz` package distributions. Checksum manifests and SBOMs should remain GitHub Release evidence assets.
reproduction: Run the release workflow against a tag that uploads one artifact containing `dist/*.whl`, `dist/*.tar.gz`, and `dist/SHA256SUMS`, then inspect the PyPI publish job.
failing_test: Add a focused release-workflow regression test that proves the checksum manifest is uploaded as a separate artifact from the PyPI distribution artifact.
impact: The release could not publish to PyPI, blocking the public v0.4.0 package even after local package build and Twine checks passed.
delivery_semantics: The fix affects release automation only and does not change runtime delivery behavior, sink writes, idempotency, DLQ handling, or ACK ordering.
security: The checksum manifest remains public release evidence. It is not a secret, but it must not be sent to PyPI as package content.
acceptance: The release workflow uploads wheel and source distributions as a package-only artifact.
The release workflow uploads `SHA256SUMS` as a separate checksum evidence artifact.
The PyPI publish job downloads only the package distribution artifact.
The GitHub Release job still attaches distributions, `SHA256SUMS`, and SBOM files.
A focused unit test guards the workflow artifact separation.
tests: Run `pytest tests/unit/test_release_checksums.py -q`, `ruff check tests/unit/test_release_checksums.py`, `python -m build`, and `twine check dist/*.whl dist/*.tar.gz`.
documentation: Update CHANGELOG.md and keep release documentation aligned with the rule that checksums and SBOMs are release evidence, not PyPI distributions.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the workflow fix has been published and the GitHub Release contains the package, checksum, and SBOM assets.
